sourceOfTruth.scope: manual screen-reader and assistive-technology review evidence

releasePolicy.alpha: Schema-valid pending scenarios are permitted when component maturity remains Experimental or Alpha Stable.
releasePolicy.beta: Every scenario covering a promoted component must have complete, passing results for all required environments.
releasePolicy.stable: Complete, passing supported-browser evidence and Accessibility owner acceptance are required in component maturity evidence.

windows-nvda-chrome.rationale: Record exact versions, reviewer, and execution date when the manual session is performed.

windows-nvda-firefox.rationale: Record exact versions, reviewer, and execution date when the manual session is performed.

scenarios.title: Button and validation announcements
contracts: Controls are announced by accessible name and role.
Disabled state is conveyed and cannot be activated.
Invalid input and its error relationship are announced without relying on color.
scenarios.rationale: VF-2014 defines the repeatable manual contract; a named reviewer must execute and record this scenario on Windows before beta promotion.

scenarios.title: Dialog and Drawer focus lifecycle
contracts: The overlay name, role, description, and modal state are announced.
Virtual and keyboard focus remain within the active modal surface.
Escape and explicit dismissal return focus to the opener.
scenarios.rationale: VF-2014 defines the repeatable manual contract; a named reviewer must execute and record this scenario on Windows before beta promotion.

scenarios.title: Menu navigation and selection
contracts: The trigger conveys that it opens a menu.
Arrow, Home, and End movement are announced in the expected item order.
Disabled, selected, and destructive item meaning is understandable.
scenarios.rationale: VF-2014 defines the repeatable manual contract; a named reviewer must execute and record this scenario on Windows before beta promotion.

scenarios.title: Popover and Tooltip relationships
contracts: Popover content is reachable without creating an unexplained focus jump.
Tooltip text is associated with its trigger and is announced once.
Dismissal does not strand focus.
scenarios.rationale: VF-2014 defines the repeatable manual contract; a named reviewer must execute and record this scenario on Windows before beta promotion.

scenarios.title: Autocomplete and MultiSelect composite controls
contracts: Expanded state, option count, active option, disabled options, and selection changes are understandable.
Typing and arrow navigation do not cause duplicate or stale announcements.
Clear and removal actions have meaningful accessible names.
scenarios.rationale: VF-2014 defines the repeatable manual contract; a named reviewer must execute and record this scenario on Windows before beta promotion.

scenarios.title: Transfer List assignment workflow
contracts: Available and assigned groups are distinguishable.
Selection and movement actions announce meaningful state changes.
Disabled items remain identifiable and protected from movement.
scenarios.rationale: VF-2014 defines the repeatable manual contract; a named reviewer must execute and record this scenario on Windows before beta promotion.

scenarios.title: Slider and Rating value changes
contracts: Range value, minimum, maximum, and increments are announced.
Rating choices expose position and checked state.
Clearing a rating produces an understandable state.
scenarios.rationale: VF-2014 defines the repeatable manual contract; a named reviewer must execute and record this scenario on Windows before beta promotion.

scenarios.title: Tabs and Toggle Button state
contracts: Tab position, selected state, disabled state, and panel relationship are announced.
Automatic activation does not produce confusing duplicate announcements.
Pressed state changes are conveyed for controlled and uncontrolled toggles.
scenarios.rationale: VF-2014 defines the repeatable manual contract; a named reviewer must execute and record this scenario on Windows before beta promotion.

scenarios.title: Toast live-region lifecycle
contracts: Polite and assertive messages are announced at the intended urgency.
Persistent and timed messages remain understandable without stealing focus.
Dismiss controls are discoverable and pause behavior does not repeat announcements.
scenarios.rationale: VF-2014 defines the repeatable manual contract; a named reviewer must execute and record this scenario on Windows before beta promotion.

scenarios.title: Data grid navigation, selection, and state
contracts: Grid name, row and column counts, headers, cell coordinates, and sort state are understandable.
Roving focus, selection, row activation, resizing, and reordering have meaningful feedback.
Empty and loading states are announced without exposing placeholder rows as real data.
scenarios.rationale: VF-2014 defines the repeatable manual contract; a named reviewer must execute and record this scenario on Windows before beta promotion.
